Examples

Sample Snippet

Workspace

The workspace containing this dataset.

virtual IWorkspace Workspace { get; }

Returns

IWorkspace

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<p>A Workspace is a container of spatial and non-spatial datasets such as feature classes, raster datasets, and tables. It provides methods to instantiate existing datasets and to create new datasets. Workspaces are classified into types specified by the <b><a href="esriWorkspaceType.htm">esriWorkspaceType</a></b> enumerator: FileSystemWorkspace, LocalDatabaseWorkspace, and RemoteDatabaseWorkspace. Shapefiles and ArcGIS for Desktop Advanced workspaces are examples of FileSystemWorkspace. A personal geodatabase stored in an Access or a File Geodatabase is an example of a LocalDatabaseWorkspace. A geodatabase stored in an RDBMS such as Oracle, DB2, SQL Server, or Informix and accessed via ArcSDE is an example of a RemoteDatabaseWorkspace.</p>&nbsp;<p>A Workspace hands out a <b><a href="WorkspaceName.htm">WorkspaceName</a>&nbsp;</b>object as the value of its FullName property. For example, the WorkspaceName for a workspace can be persisted in a map document. An application can call the <b>Open()</b> method on the workspace name after loading it from persistent storage to connect to and get an object reference to the workspace.</p>

WorkspaceFactory

The factory that created the workspace.

virtual IWorkspaceFactory WorkspaceFactory { get; }

Returns

IWorkspaceFactory

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<CODE> <P>A WorkspaceFactory is a dispenser of workspaces and allows a client to connect to a workspace specified by a set of connection properties. A workspace represents a database or a datasource that contains one or more datasets. Examples of datasets include tables, feature classes and relationship classes. A WorkspaceFactory is a cocreatable, singleton object. A WorkspaceFactory maintains a pool of currently connected, active workspaces that are being referenced by the application. Connection properties are specified using a PropertySet object and can be saved to a connection file. A WorkspaceFactory also supports methods that can be used to browse and manage file system workspaces, and methods to manage connection files for remote database workspaces.</P></CODE>

Made with docfx
